title: Remove the controller PCA assembly access components and release the controller box
summary: Safely prepare for controller PCA replacement by gathering the specified tools and PPE, locking out/tagging out the tipper, removing the station side panel if needed for access, loosening the controller box mounting screws without removing them, and lifting the controller box off the loosened fasteners.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when performing the removal/access portion of Controller PCA replacement and access to the controller box is required.
not_for: Do not use this runbook as a complete Controller PCA replacement procedure; the supplied source section is removal-only and does not include the actual PCA swap or reinstallation steps.
Do not use this procedure if lockout/tagout cannot be completed safely.

responsibilities: Gather the specified replacement part, PPE, and tools.
Perform lockout/tagout of the tipper per the referenced operator station lockout/tagout procedure.
Remove access components if needed and release the controller box using the documented fasteners.

safety_notes: LOTO the tipper before performing removal steps.
Do not remove the four M8 button-head screws when releasing the controller box; only loosen them.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the station side panel and controller box

access_required: Access to the station side panel and controller box
system_preconditions: Tipper must be locked out/tagged out before removal work begins.

visual_references.description: Figure 7-19 Controller PCA identifying the controller PCA and controller box location in the Controller PCA Replacement procedure.
Related controller box visual showing controller/controller box location and the four M8 button-head screw release pattern.

steps.title: Gather required replacement part, PPE, and tools
steps.purpose: Ensure all required materials and tools are available before beginning controller PCA access and removal work.
steps.instruction: Gather the replacement Controller PCA, safety glasses, gloves, a 1.5-mm hex wrench, a 6-mm hex wrench, and a 3/8-in. wrench or socket.
steps.expected_result: All listed PPE, tools, and the replacement Controller PCA are available at the work area.
steps.healthy_condition: The technician has the specified replacement part and all listed tools and PPE before starting the procedure.
steps.failure_condition: One or more required tools, PPE items, or the replacement Controller PCA are unavailable.

actions.label: Verify required items
actions.exact_action: Gather the replacement Controller PCA, safety glasses, gloves, a 1.5-mm hex wrench, a 6-mm hex wrench, and a 3/8-in. wrench or socket.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the figure to confirm the component being serviced is the Controller PCA/controller box assembly.

stop_or_escalate_if: The replacement Controller PCA is not available or cannot be confirmed.

source_evidence.text: Gather the replacement Controller PCA, safety glasses, gloves, a 1.5-mm hex wrench, a 6-mm hex wrench, and a 3/8-in. wrench or socket.
Nearby procedure text states required tools include a 1.5-mm hex wrench, 6-mm hex wrench, and 3/8-in. wrench or socket, with replacement part Controller PCA and PPE of safety glasses and gloves.

steps.title: Lock out and tag out the tipper
steps.purpose: Place the equipment in a safe state before accessing internal components and loosening controller box fasteners.
steps.instruction: LOTO the tipper as referenced by "Operator Station Lockout/Tagout" on page 2.
steps.expected_result: The tipper is locked out/tagged out and safe for maintenance access.
steps.healthy_condition: LOTO is completed per the referenced operator station lockout/tagout procedure before any access or removal work continues.
steps.failure_condition: LOTO cannot be completed safely or the tipper remains in an unsafe state for maintenance.

actions.label: Apply LOTO
actions.exact_action: LOTO the tipper as referenced by "Operator Station Lockout/Tagout" on page 2.

stop_or_escalate_if: The referenced lockout/tagout procedure is unavailable.
The tipper cannot be verified in a safe maintenance state.

source_evidence.text: LOTO the tipper as referenced by "Operator Station Lockout/Tagout" on page 2.
Removal steps instruct the technician to LOTO the tipper.
Stop if lockout/tagout cannot be completed safely.

steps.title: Remove the station side panel if access is needed
steps.purpose: Gain access to the internal area needed to reach the controller box and related components.
steps.instruction: If necessary to gain access, use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the station side panel, then remove the station side panel to access the motor.
steps.expected_result: The station side panel is removed when needed, providing access to the internal service area.
steps.healthy_condition: The side panel is removed cleanly using the documented nine self-tapping fasteners and access to the internal area is available.
steps.failure_condition: The station side panel cannot be accessed or removed using the documented fasteners and tool.

actions.label: Remove side panel fasteners
actions.exact_action: Use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the station side panel.

actions.label: Remove station side panel
actions.exact_action: Remove the station side panel to access the motor.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look for the controller PCA/controller box service area associated with this procedure.

stop_or_escalate_if: The station side panel cannot be accessed.
The documented nine self-tapping fasteners cannot be removed with the specified tool.
Removing the side panel does not provide the expected access.

source_evidence.text: If necessary to gain access, use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the station side panel, then remove the station side panel to access the motor.

Removal steps instruct the technician to LOTO the tipper, remove nine self-tapping fasteners from the station side panel if needed, and loosen four M8 button-head screws to release the controller box.
Stop if the station side panel or controller box cannot be accessed or released using the documented fasteners and tools.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-19 appears on page 177 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_18_1_removal.

steps.title: Loosen the controller box mounting screws
steps.purpose: Release the controller box from its mounting points without fully removing the fasteners.
steps.instruction: Loosen the four M8 button-head screws to release the controller box; do not remove the fasteners.
steps.expected_result: The controller box is released from the mounting points while the fasteners remain in place.
steps.healthy_condition: All four M8 button-head screws are loosened enough to release the controller box, and none of the fasteners are removed.
steps.failure_condition: The controller box does not release after loosening the documented screws, or the fasteners cannot be loosened as described.

actions.label: Loosen controller box screws
actions.exact_action: Loosen the four M8 button-head screws to release the controller box; do not remove the fasteners.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Identify the Controller PCA/controller box and the mounting area associated with the four M8 button-head screws.
Use the related controller box figure as supplemental visual guidance for controller box location and release fastener context.

stop_or_escalate_if: The four M8 button-head screws cannot be identified.
The screws cannot be loosened using the documented approach.
The controller box does not release after loosening the documented fasteners.
A fastener would need to be removed contrary to the source instruction.

source_evidence.text: Loosen the four M8 button-head screws to release the controller box; do not remove the fasteners.

Removal steps instruct the technician to LOTO the tipper, remove nine self-tapping fasteners from the station side panel if needed, and loosen four M8 button-head screws to release the controller box.

The procedure instructs technicians to LOTO the tipper, remove the station side panel if needed, loosen four M8 button-head screws to release the controller box, and disconnect the cables connected to the CLINK controller.
Stop if the station side panel or controller box cannot be accessed or released using the documented fasteners and tools.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-19 appears on page 177 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_18_1_removal.

steps.title: Lift the controller box off the loosened fasteners
steps.purpose: Complete the release of the controller box so controller PCA replacement work can proceed.
steps.instruction: Lift the controller box off the loosened fasteners.
steps.expected_result: The controller box is free of the loosened fasteners and accessible for the next stage of controller PCA replacement work.
steps.healthy_condition: The controller box lifts off the loosened fasteners without requiring undocumented disassembly.
steps.failure_condition: The controller box cannot be lifted off the loosened fasteners or additional undocumented constraints prevent removal.

actions.label: Lift controller box free
actions.exact_action: Lift the controller box off the loosened fasteners.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Confirm controller box location and mounting relationship before lifting it off the loosened fasteners.
Use as supplemental visual reference for controller box position and release orientation.

stop_or_escalate_if: The controller box cannot be lifted off the loosened fasteners.
Additional undocumented fasteners or obstructions are encountered.
The controller box cannot be accessed or released using the documented fasteners and tools.

source_evidence.text: Lift the controller box off the loosened fasteners.
The tipper is locked out, the station side panel is removed if needed for access, and the controller box is released and can be lifted off its loosened fasteners for controller PCA replacement work.
Stop if the station side panel or controller box cannot be accessed or released using the documented fasteners and tools.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-19 appears on page 177 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_18_1_removal.

success_criteria: The required PPE, tools, and replacement Controller PCA are gathered.
The tipper is locked out/tagged out.
The station side panel is removed if needed for access.
The four M8 button-head screws are loosened without being removed.
The controller box is released and can be lifted off the loosened fasteners.
healthy_conditions: LOTO is completed before internal access work begins.
The station side panel can be removed using the documented nine self-tapping fasteners when access is needed.
The controller box releases after loosening the four M8 button-head screws.
The controller box lifts off the loosened fasteners without undocumented extra steps.
failure_conditions: The station side panel cannot be accessed or removed using the documented fasteners and tools.
The controller box cannot be accessed or released using the documented fasteners and tools.
The source section is incomplete for the full Controller PCA replacement beyond removal/access.
escalation_guidance: Stop and escalate if lockout/tagout cannot be completed safely.
Stop and escalate if the station side panel or controller box cannot be accessed or released using the documented fasteners and tools.
Escalate for additional source material or SME guidance for the actual PCA swap and reinstallation because the supplied source is removal-only.
